findings.name: Telekinetic Savant
findings.mechanic: Swift Action recovery path already counts owned Telekinetic Savant selections as per-encounter uses and recovers one spent [Telekinetic] Force Power without spending a Force Point.
findings.pickerDescription: Once per encounter as a Swift Action, you may return one Force Power with the [Telekinetic] descriptor to your suite without spending a Force Point. You may take this Talent multiple times. Each time you select it, you may use this Talent one additional time per encounter.
evidence: Force suite UI Telekinetic Savant picker and descriptor guard

findings.name: Telekinetic Prodigy
findings.mechanic: The Force Power selection step suppresses the old Prodigy-only retroactive slot trigger. Prodigy grants its extra [Telekinetic] slot only when the current Force Training selection includes Move Object.
findings.pickerDescription: When you take the Force Training Feat and select Move Object as one of your Force Powers, you can also select one extra Force Power to add to your Force Power Suite for free. This extra Force Power must contain the [Telekinetic] descriptor. You can only gain one extra Force Power each time you take the Force Training Feat, regardless of how many times you choose the Move Object Force Power. If you increase your Wisdom score at a later time and select the Move Object power, you only gain an additional power if you did not take the Move Object power when you first chose that Feat. Otherwise, you gain an additional Force Power as normal.

pickerHydration.reason: The detail rail prefers normalized talent payload data before fallback helpers, so the talent payload itself is corrected before picker rows, details, and committed selections read it.

automationBoundary.Telekinetic Savant: Fully wired for force-suite recovery workflow; still depends on power cards accurately tagging [Telekinetic] powers.
automationBoundary.Telekinetic Prodigy: Corrects current runtime over-granting. The exact later-Wisdom/same-Force-Training historical caveat requires per-Force-Training selection provenance if that is not already stored. This fix prevents a talent-pick-only Prodigy slot and keeps the slot tied to current Force Training + Move Object selection.
